name: Horizontal Scaling with Sharding
description: Distribute data across multiple shards for horizontal scaling using MongoDB sharded clusters with config servers and mongos routers.
trendingReason: Sharding is critical for scaling MongoDB beyond single-server limits in high-growth applications

steps.title: Start the config server replica set
steps.description: Launch config server instances that store cluster metadata and routing information.
steps.tip: In production, run 3 config server members for redundancy. A single member works for testing.

steps.title: Initialize config server replica set
steps.description: Connect to the config server and initiate the replica set.

steps.title: Start a mongos router
steps.description: Launch the query router that applications connect to for routing queries to the correct shards.

steps.title: Add shards to the cluster
steps.description: Register shard replica sets with the cluster through the mongos router.

steps.title: Enable sharding on a database and shard a collection
steps.description: Choose a shard key and enable sharding for a specific collection.
steps.warning: The shard key cannot be changed after sharding. Choose carefully based on your query patterns and data distribution.

steps.title: Check sharding status
steps.description: View the overall sharding topology, chunk distribution, and balancer status.

faq.question: How do I choose a good shard key?
faq.answer: A good shard key has high cardinality (many unique values), even distribution, and matches your most common query patterns. Hashed shard keys distribute evenly but do not support range queries. Compound shard keys can balance distribution with query efficiency.

faq.question: Can I change the shard key after sharding?
faq.answer: MongoDB 5.0+ supports resharding with sh.reshardCollection(), but it is a heavy operation that copies all data. In earlier versions, you must dump the collection, drop it, recreate it with a new shard key, and reimport.

faq.question: What is the balancer and should it always run?
faq.answer: The balancer automatically migrates chunks between shards to maintain even distribution. It should generally be enabled, but you can schedule it to run during off-peak hours with sh.setBalancerState() and balancer windows to avoid performance impact during peak traffic.
